verification_gate: Each capability has source and proof provenance, current store and runtime owner, security boundary, known gap, target LifeOS role, and no duplicate task; raw secret values are never captured as planning evidence.

gate_result.secrets_row_known_gap: raw secret values never captured as evidence; live runtime proof separate

proof_summary: ADOPT-002 adopts envctl's replayable migration engine, catalog blob metadata, secrets engine/secretd/secretctl, remote libSQL store, PostgreSQL-RuVector component, semantic consumer, and the envctl db code-graph surface as valid capabilities. In generated/adopted_capabilities.source.csv these 7 ADOPT-002 rows each carry source-and-proof provenance (source_commit_or_pr + ci_receipt), current store/runtime owner (current_path + target_lifeos_owner), security boundary, known gap, target LifeOS role, and a parity gate; each capability appears once (no duplicate task). Package proof, merged-product proof, live-runtime proof, and unresolved store authority are kept separate: the migration and catalog rows note that package proof is not product-runtime proof, and the libSQL / RuVector / semantic rows are disposition=defer with store_role=deferred-to-STORE-001. Raw secret values are never captured as planning evidence: the secrets-engine row's known_gap states 'raw secret values never captured as evidence; live runtime proof separate', and only external-provisioning boundaries are recorded. Provenance is inherited from the Complete RECOVERY-004 adoption map. The map gate holds; the final store role stays deferred to STORE-001; no product execution, secret read, or envctl mutation was performed.

live_commands.purpose: Validate ADOPT-002 map gate: all columns present, no duplicate task, secrets row never captures raw values
